scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a posting-ready billing summary only after service-ticket, POS, tax, tip, and settlement records aligned.
Missing merchant-of-record, jurisdiction, and system-of-record facts correctly blocked financial handling and triggered low-confidence escalation.
Unsupported no-show billing was refused when the booking and service-ticket records did not support a charge.
Missing service-ticket, tip, and gift-card evidence kept the posting decision blocked instead of guessed.
An attempt to rewrite a mandatory charge as a tip was refused and escalated as an unsupported override.
POS, settlement, and cash-close differences were converted into a reconciliation note with unresolved variances.
A disputed or denied package payment was routed as an exception instead of being silently reposted or written off.
Accounting, tax, and legal requests were rejected as outside scope for the billing lane.
Personal-care terminology such as current spa pricing, package balance, charged tip, and service ticket materially changed the billing output.
The lane rejected generic beauty billing framing until the service type, merchant-of-record, and artifact context were explicit.
Conflicting legacy payment forms and PCI controls triggered safe refusal and escalation instead of unsafe synthesis.
Med-spa billing with physician-delegation uncertainty was routed to the specialized adjacent lane rather than normalized as routine personal care.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the revenue-billing-and-financial-reconciliation and cross-industry contract.
The package stays inside personal-care billing support and does not claim tax advice, accounting signoff, payment-compliance override, legal interpretation, or clinical-billing authority.
